program_name: Economics for Management Students Major (B.Com.) (69 credits)
description: The B.Com.; Major in Economics for Management Students is a planned sequence of courses designed to permit a degree of specialization in economics, including microeconomics (focusing on the study of the behaviour of individual economic agents and how the interaction of individuals results in market outcomes) and macroeconomics (focusing on economy-wide issues such as unemployment rates, money supply and inflation, as well as public policies to influence such macroeconomic aggregates). Topics include: econometrics, economic history, economic development, environmental economics, industrial organization, international trade and finance, labour economics, money and banking, and public finance.
